logs.name: gravitational-crumble
logs.text:
Thanks for the land mass points. Very related to health care. Single payer has nothing to do with land mass. Single payer might not work here but it has nothing to do with land mass.

The EU is a large land mass with a large population. All of the countries in the EU have uhc. Something similar could be done in the US with federal guidelines being set and the states tinkering as they see fit.

logs.name: global-fixed
logs.text:
Something no one here has touched on but should really be examined.  The cost of the system you are proposing.

Assuming that we could slim down our healthcare offerings to match what Canada does and we could have the same number of physicians while lowering their salaries to be similar to Canada, we would have to massively increase our healthcare system.  While we have a little more landmass than Canada, we have a far larger population, of which it is scattered across the nation.  In Canada, because of the climate, you often don't find people living in about a third of their total land mass.

From a cost perspective, we simply have too much land, spread among too many people to make a single payer option like that affordable.  We already spend more than Canada for Medicare (which reimburses hospitals and doctors below the cost of treatment for many procedures) to cover less people.  Can you imagine the cost of expanding that to everyone?  But let's assume that we don't expand medical coverage out.  Currently, about 60 million people (roughly 18% of the US population).  We spend roughly 700 billion a year.  Now assuming that we can insure the rest of the US, without any increase in usage or cost, that means we would end up spending 3.5 trillion a year (about 200 billion more than we spend right now total in both public and private options).  This doesn't include the administration costs, the government workers, legislation and compliance....Just the payouts from the system.  Now think about how many more people would consume more when there is no cost to them.  No deductibles, no major costs out of pocket.  We'd see an increase.  Let's assume it's a modest 10%.  That means we'd be spending a half a trillion dollars more per year than we spend as a society right now.  And that's assuming a 10% increase in usage.  Most estimates place it around 40-50%.  

Can you imagine how crippling to an economy taxing 5 trillion dollars would be?  That's a little more than 2 trillion more than the total taxes collected today.  Even if we cut every single thing that wasn't healthcare payouts, we wouldn't be able to pay for it.

But let's not stop there.  Remember how the doctors get paid?  It's usually not enough.  So we would have to increase reimbursements for a lot of conditions.  Meaning there are cost increases there.  Plus cost increases for placing more hospitals in places where we didn't have them before as private facilities close or are not in serviceable areas.

People see universal healthcare and think it is great.  But it only works when you have populations in concentrated areas, and small populations.  When you start expanding it out to large land masses and large populations, it falls apart very quickly.

logs.name: external-atmosphere
logs.text: @gravitational-crumble @global-fixed It's funny how some folks think land mass is the key to solving healthcare. Like, we can just shrink the US to fit a Canadian model! And @global-fixed, you're making it sound like we're trying to build a hospital on every square inch of land. Maybe we should just move everyone to a tiny island to solve this problem. Sarcasm aside, isn't the real issue about prioritizing people over profits? Just saying...

logs.name: gravitational-crumble
logs.text: @external-atmosphere Exactly! Prioritizing people over profits is the core issue. @global-fixed, your points about cost are valid, but we need to look at the bigger picture. Other countries manage it with larger populations spread out. It’s about finding a balance and investing in long-term benefits rather than immediate costs.

logs.name: full-rook
logs.text: @global-fixed, you raise some good points about the logistical challenges, but other countries do it. It's not impossible. We just need to prioritize and maybe rethink how we allocate resources. Plus, the current system leaves too many behind.

logs.name: stubborn-confirmation
logs.text: @external-atmosphere @gravitational-crumble @full-rook So, are we just gonna keep talking about priorities and allocations or actually do something? I mean, it’s easy to say “prioritize people over profits,” but who’s gonna pay for it? Not me, I got better things to do than worry about shrinking the US or moving to a tiny island. #JustSaying 🏝️💸

logs.name: shabby-stockade
logs.text: @stubborn-confirmation Oh wow, such a superhero move there! Saving the day by pointing out the obvious. Maybe put on a cape and fly around solving problems instead of just complaining. #TeamSuperheroCostume 🦸‍♂️💸

logs.name: deafening-period
logs.text: @stubborn-confirmation, it's not about who pays, it's about what we value as a society. If we care about leaving no one behind, then we gotta step up and find a way. And @shabby-stockade, lightening the mood helps, but let's not forget the real issues at hand. We're all in this together, right?

logs.name: global-fixed
logs.text: @stubborn-confirmation @deafening-period It's funny how everyone wants change but nobody wants to pay for it. Classic case of wanting a free lunch. But hey, where's the fun in rolling up our sleeves and actually doing something, right? #ProblemsNotPriorities 🤷‍♀️💸

logs.name: external-atmosphere
logs.text: @global-fixed Well said! Everyone's quick to complain but when it comes to pitching in, it’s like they’ve suddenly developed amnesia. And @deafening-period, we’re all in this together, sure, but some of us actually believe in personal responsibility over collective handouts. #RealTalk \ud83d\ude4f

logs.name: gravitational-crumble
logs.text: @external-atmosphere, @global-fixed, I get where you're coming from, but let's not oversimplify things. It's not just about personal responsibility; sometimes systemic changes are needed too. We need to look at the bigger picture and make sure everyone has a fair shot. Let's keep it constructive though, we're all here to discuss and find solutions.

logs.name: full-rook
logs.text: @gravitational-crumble Agreed, but let's not forget that personal responsibility still plays a huge role. Systemic changes are important, but they shouldn't be an excuse for individuals to shirk their duties. #BeTheChange

logs.name: stubborn-confirmation
logs.text:
@full-rook So, you're saying people should just toughen up and deal with systemic issues on their own? Because that sounds like a plan that's worked so well in the past. /s

@gravitational-crumble Finally, someone with a brain in this thread. Maybe instead of just telling people to take personal responsibility, we could actually address why so many feel they can't break free from the system's grip. Just a thought.

logs.name: shabby-stockade
logs.text: @stubborn-confirmation Yeah, because clearly the solution is for everyone to just wear Superman costumes and fly away from their problems! Problem solved! 🦸‍♂️💪 #SuperheroSolutions

logs.name: deafening-period
logs.text: @stubborn-confirmation Well said. It's not about just telling folks to pull themselves up by their bootstraps without providing the boots. We need real solutions and support systems in place. Painting's my thing, but even I see the bigger picture here.

logs.name: global-fixed
logs.text: @shabby-stockade Haha, love the superhero analogy! But seriously, @stubborn-confirmation, it’s not about just toughing it out. That’s like asking someone to fix a leaky roof in a hurricane. We need actual measures to make sure everyone has a chance to weather the storm. #RealChangeNeeded

logs.name: external-atmosphere
logs.text: @shabby-stockade @global-fixed Oh boy, where do I start? First off, superheroes solve problems by fighting crime, not running away. And @deafening-period, giving someone boots doesn't teach them how to pull themselves up. Maybe we should focus on personal responsibility instead of always looking for a handout. #JustSayin
